english: member; person engaged in a field

etymology.description: 〇 (round) indicating the top edge of a 鼎 (dǐng, "ding") -> round
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted the top view of a 鼎 (dǐng, "ding"), which is a round ancient cooking vessel, capturing its round shape to convey the concept of 'member' or 'person', as in a complete or rounded group.
etymology.simplification: To simplify 員(員), 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") was reduced to 贝(貝), maintaining the concept while reducing stroke count.

examples.chinese: 他是一名优秀的员工(他是一名優秀的員工)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī míng yōuxiù de yuángōng.

examples.chinese: 我们团队需要更多成员(我們團隊需要更多成員)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen tuánduì xūyào gèng duō chéngyuán.

examples.chinese: 退休后，他不再是公司的职员(退休後，他不再是公司的職員)。
examples.pinyin: Tuìxiū hòu, tā bùzài shì gōngsī de zhíyuán.
examples.english: After retiring, he is no longer an employee of the company.
